title: Brand Marketing Lead (m/w/d)
summary: Candidate has relevant FinTech and campaign analytics experience that partially aligns with the role, but the position is fundamentally a brand leadership role requiring native German, 5+ years of brand-specific experience, and team management — areas where the CV shows significant gaps. The profile is analytics- and data-oriented rather than brand-creative, and German proficiency at B1+ falls well short of the native-level requirement.
required_skills: 5+ years in brand, marketing or campaign roles (strategic + operational)
missing_skills: Native-level German (role explicitly requires Muttersprache Deutsch; CV shows B1+)
Brand strategy ownership and positioning experience
Brand governance and brand guidelines management
core_responsibilities: Own brand strategy, positioning and messaging
Develop and execute cross-channel campaigns (web, social, CRM, paid)
Maintain brand consistency across all touchpoints
Measure and optimize brand KPIs (awareness, engagement, consideration, conversion)
Lead a small team and collaborate with performance, CRM, product and data functions
recommended_emphasis: Highlight FinTech experience at HSBC (digital transformation, C-suite reporting) to demonstrate industry familiarity
Emphasize campaign analytics and ROI measurement work at Intelligent Power and GRG Banking as evidence of campaign execution understanding
Showcase cross-functional collaboration skills and project management credentials (Scrum, SAFe)
Frame Marketing Manager and Product Marketing Manager roles as brand-adjacent experience with strategic input
Stress any multilingual content or cross-market communication work to partially address the language dimension
risk_flags: German language gap: role requires native-level German (Muttersprache), CV shows B1+ — likely a hard screening filter
Experience shortfall: JD requires 5+ years in brand/marketing roles; CV total is ~4 years and not primarily brand-focused
No team leadership evidence: JD requires leading a small team; CV shows no people management experience
Brand vs. analytics mismatch: candidate profile is data/analytics-oriented, not brand-creative; this is a core brand leadership role
No media planning background evident in CV
